intro: Agent CI aims to run real GitHub Actions workflows locally. The table below shows current support against the [official workflow syntax](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/using-workflows/workflow-syntax-for-github-actions).

supported.label: Supported
supported.description: Fully implemented and behaves like GitHub Actions

partial.label: Partial
partial.description: Works with limitations — see notes for details

unsupported.label: Not supported
unsupported.description: Not implemented — the feature is skipped or errors

ignored.label: Ignored (no-op)
ignored.description: Parsed without error but has no effect on the run

not-planned.label: Not planned
not-planned.description: Cannot be supported due to architectural constraints

sections.title: Workflow-Level Keys
sections.description: Top-level keys that control when and how a workflow runs — triggers, environment defaults, and concurrency.

rows.notes: Parsed but not displayed anywhere
Branch and path filters are evaluated when using `--all`
Accepted without error, but Agent CI does not simulate event triggers — workflows must be run manually

Local reusable workflows (`uses: ./.github/workflows/...`) are inlined into the caller's dependency graph. Remote refs are fetched from GitHub (requires `--github-token` or `AGENT_CI_GITHUB_TOKEN`). `inputs:`/`outputs:` passing and nested reusable workflows are supported
Parsed without error, but the event is not simulated
Workflow-level env is propagated to all steps
Passed through to the runner
Passed through to the runner
Accepted but not enforced — the mock GITHUB_TOKEN has full access
Concurrency groups are a GitHub-side queuing and cancellation mechanism. Agent CI has no persistent server to track group state across runs, so this cannot be implemented locally

sections.title: Job-Level Keys
sections.description: Per-job configuration — runner selection, dependencies, conditional execution, containers, and service sidecars.

rows.notes: Multiple jobs in a single workflow
Jobs are sorted topologically into dependency waves
`success()`, `failure()`, `always()`, `cancelled()`, `==`/`!=`, `&&`/`||`, `!`, `needs.*.outputs.*`, `needs.*.result`

`ubuntu-*` / `linux` (and unspecified `self-hosted` / custom labels) run in a Linux container — the default image is minimal, see [runner-image.md](./runner-image.md) to add system tools. `macos-*` runs in a real macOS VM on Apple Silicon hosts with [tart](https://github.com/cirruslabs/tart) + `sshpass` installed; other hosts skip the job with a reason. `windows-*` is not yet supported and skips on every host.
Accepted but not enforced — environment protection rules are GitHub-side only
`shell` and `working-directory`
Resolved after each job completes and accumulated across dependency waves
Not implemented. Agent CI's pause-on-failure model is the intended way to handle long-running steps — a hard timeout would destroy the container state that makes local debugging possible
Not implemented. Agent CI pauses on failure so you can inspect and fix the container in place; `continue-on-error` would skip past failures and discard that debugging opportunity
See workflow-level `concurrency` above
Short and long form; image, env, ports, volumes, and options are all supported
Sidecar containers with image, env, ports, and options

Local refs (`./`) are expanded inline. Remote refs are fetched from GitHub (requires `--github-token` or `AGENT_CI_GITHUB_TOKEN`; public repos may work without auth). `with:` (inputs) and `secrets:` pass-through are supported

Agent CI cannot access GitHub's secret storage. Secrets are resolved from a `.env.agent-ci` file at the project root or from shell environment variables as fallback. `--github-token` / `AGENT_CI_GITHUB_TOKEN` auto-populates `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`. All are injected as `${{ secrets.* }}` expressions

sections.title: Strategy / Matrix
sections.description: Matrix builds and failure behavior — how jobs are multiplied across configurations and when to stop on errors.

rows.notes: Cartesian product of all array values is fully expanded
Not implemented. The matrix parser only processes array-valued keys; `include` entries (which are objects) are silently dropped. Adding support would require post-processing the Cartesian product
Not implemented — same reason as `include`. `exclude` entries are objects and are dropped by the array-only parser
Setting `fail-fast: false` allows remaining matrix jobs to continue after a failure
Not implemented. Parallelism is controlled by Agent CI's host-level concurrency limiter (based on CPU count), not per-workflow job limits

sections.title: Step-Level Keys
sections.description: Individual step configuration — shell commands, action references, conditional execution, and environment variables.

rows.notes: Expression expansion in names

The condition is passed to the official runner binary, which evaluates it at runtime. Limitation: `steps.*.outputs.cache-hit` and similar outputs resolve to an empty string at parse time because prior steps have not yet run when the workflow is parsed
Multiline shell scripts with `${{ }}` expression expansion
Public actions are downloaded via the GitHub API
Local actions (e.g. `uses: ./.github/actions/my-action`) are resolved from the workspace
Expression expansion in values
Expression expansion in values
Passed through to the runner
Not implemented — see `jobs.<id>.continue-on-error` above for the reasoning
Not implemented — see `jobs.<id>.timeout-minutes` above for the reasoning

sections.title: Expressions (`${{ }}`)
sections.description: The `${{ }}` expression language — built-in functions, context variables, and operators available in workflow YAML.

rows.notes: SHA-256 of matching files; supports multiple glob patterns
Template substitution with recursive expression expansion
Resolved from `.env.agent-ci` at the project root, with shell environment variables as fallback. `--github-token` auto-populates `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`
`Linux` for container-based jobs, `macOS` for jobs routed to the macOS VM
`X64` for container-based jobs, `ARM64` for macOS VM jobs (Apple Silicon)

`sha` is the real commit SHA from `HEAD`. When the working tree is dirty, a temporary tree SHA is computed to reflect uncommitted changes. Other values use defaults: `ref_name` and `head_ref` are `main`, `repository` is derived from the git remote, `actor` is the repo owner, `run_id` and `run_number` are `1`
All event payload fields return empty strings — no real webhook event is triggered locally
Resolves to an empty string at parse time. The official runner evaluates these correctly at runtime — the limitation only affects Agent CI's own expression pre-processing
Resolved after dependency jobs complete. The needs context is built from actual job outputs and passed into subsequent job evaluation
`==`, `!=`, `<`, `>`, `<=`, `>=`, `&&`, `||`, `!`, parentheses. Case-insensitive string comparison; numeric comparison when both sides are numbers
Case-insensitive. `contains` works with both strings and JSON arrays
Joins JSON arrays with a separator (default `, `)
Evaluated by Agent CI for job-level `if` conditions and in expression expansion
Boolean, null, and numeric literals supported in expressions

sections.title: GitHub API Features (DTU Mock)
sections.description: Interactions with GitHub's API — action downloads, common actions (cache, checkout, setup-*), artifacts, and tokens.

rows.notes: Action tarballs are resolved and downloaded from github.com
Cache is stored on the local filesystem via bind-mount, giving ~0 ms round-trip on cache hits
The workspace is rsynced into the container with `clean: false` to preserve local changes
Tool setup actions run natively inside the runner container
Artifacts are stored on the local filesystem

Not injected automatically. Pass `--github-token` or set `AGENT_CI_GITHUB_TOKEN` to make it available as `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN`. GitHub API calls from the runner are answered locally by Agent CI's API emulation layer
Handled by the official runner binary
